task:
I am not so sure I want to verify these baselines on staging when we can collect like-for-like baselines on local dev:
1 - Before all optimizations
2 - after SDK layer optimizations
3 - after kubo optimizations

this should give us enough clarity on what the general trend is based on these changes, without unnecessary destructive staging deployments.

task:
ok, so I think the order of operations is as follows:

1. create PR, handle reviews, merge, create new release, merge release, prepare staging deployment.
2. ssh to staging, disable api container to ensure no new data is written to ipfs
3 -> 7. are 1-> 5 from above 
8. delete old kubo volume entirely
9. approve staging deployment, let the deployment script do its thing and create a blank kubo
10. stop everything, attach the migrated bebbleds datastore to the new kubo container and restart everything
user_feedback_text:
ok, so I think the order of operations is as follows:

1. create PR, handle reviews, merge, create new release, merge release, prepare staging deployment.
2. ssh to staging, disable api container to ensure no new data is written to ipfs
3 -> 7. are 1-> 5 from above 
8. delete old kubo volume entirely
9. approve staging deployment, let the deployment script do its thing and create a blank kubo
10. stop everything, attach the migrated bebbleds datastore to the new kubo container and restart everything
